skill.name: copilot-sdk
skill.description: Build applications powered by GitHub Copilot using the Copilot SDK with session management, custom tools, hooks, and MCP server integration.
skill.summary: Official GitHub Copilot SDK documentation for programmatic AI integration across Node.js, Python, Go, and .NET.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 192 static analysis findings are false positives. The scanner misinterpreted markdown code block delimiters (```) as shell backtick execution. The file contains documentation examples only, not executable code. URLs are reference links, environment variable mentions document authentication patterns. No actual security risks detected.

content.user_title: Build AI Apps with GitHub Copilot SDK
content.value_statement: Developers need programmatic access to GitHub Copilot for custom AI workflows. This SDK provides session management, custom tools, hooks, and MCP integration across Node.js, Python, Go, and .NET.
actual_capabilities: Create and manage Copilot sessions with custom models and configurations
Define custom tools that Copilot can call during conversations
Implement hooks for tool permission control and lifecycle events
Connect to MCP servers for extended tool capabilities
Enable streaming responses for real-time output
Support BYOK (Bring Your Own Key) for custom AI providers
limitations: Requires GitHub Copilot CLI installation and authentication
Only supports Node.js 18+, Python 3.8+, Go 1.21+, and .NET 8.0+
Custom agents and skills require local file system access
Session persistence requires manual session ID management

use_cases.title: AI-Powered Development Tools
use_cases.description: Build IDE extensions or CLI tools that leverage Copilot for code generation, review, and refactoring with custom tool integrations.

use_cases.title: Automated Code Review Systems
use_cases.description: Create custom agents for pull request analysis, security scanning, and best practices validation with automated feedback.

use_cases.title: Multi-Agent Workflows
use_cases.description: Orchestrate specialized AI agents for different tasks like documentation, testing, and deployment using MCP server integration.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Session Creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a CopilotClient, start a session with the gpt-4.1 model, and send a message asking for code explanation.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with basic Copilot SDK usage

prompt_templates.title: Custom Tool Integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Define a custom tool that retrieves file contents from the filesystem, then create a session that can use this tool to answer questions about project files.

prompt_templates.title: Streaming with Event Handlers
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up a streaming session that displays real-time responses, handles assistant message deltas, and logs session idle events.

prompt_templates.title: MCP Server and Hooks
prompt_templates.prompt: Configure a session with an MCP server for GitHub API access, add hooks to control shell command permissions, and implement custom error handling.
prompt_templates.scenario: Production-ready AI application with security controls

output_examples.input: Create a session and ask Copilot to explain a Python function
output_examples.output: Session created with gpt-4.1 model. Copilot analyzes the function and provides a detailed explanation of its purpose, parameters, return value, and potential improvements.

output_examples.input: Define a weather tool and ask Copilot about conditions in a city
output_examples.output: Custom tool registered. When asked about weather, Copilot calls the tool and returns structured data including city, temperature, and conditions.

output_examples.input: Enable streaming and request a code review
output_examples.output: Response streams token-by-token in real-time. Code review feedback appears progressively with syntax highlighting and inline suggestions.

best_practices: Use hooks to implement permission controls for sensitive operations like shell commands and file access
Enable debug logging during development to troubleshoot connection and authentication issues
Use session persistence with custom IDs for long-running workflows that may exceed context limits
anti_patterns: Hardcoding GitHub tokens in source code instead of using environment variables
Creating new sessions for every request instead of reusing sessions for related tasks
Ignoring permission requests and auto-approving all tool calls without validation

faq.question: What is the difference between Copilot SDK and Copilot CLI?
faq.answer: The CLI is a command-line interface for direct user interaction. The SDK wraps the CLI to enable programmatic access for building applications that integrate Copilot capabilities.

faq.question: Do I need a GitHub Copilot subscription to use the SDK?
faq.answer: A subscription is required for standard GitHub Copilot models. However, you can use BYOK (Bring Your Own Key) to connect to alternative providers like Azure OpenAI or Anthropic without a Copilot subscription.

faq.question: How do I handle authentication for the SDK?
faq.answer: Authentication priority: 1) Explicit githubToken in constructor, 2) COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN environment variable, 3) GH_TOKEN or GITHUB_TOKEN, 4) Stored OAuth from copilot auth login, 5) GitHub CLI credentials.

faq.question: Can I use the SDK with local AI models?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the BYOK provider configuration with Ollama or other local model servers. Set provider type to 'openai' and specify your local endpoint URL.

faq.question: What are MCP servers and how do I use them?
faq.answer: MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers provide pre-built tools via standardized interfaces. Configure mcpServers in session config with either HTTP URLs for remote servers or local command configurations for stdio servers.

faq.question: How do I prevent unauthorized tool execution?
faq.answer: Use the onPreToolUse hook to intercept tool calls. Return permissionDecision: 'deny' with a reason for blocked operations. Auto-approve only trusted tools like git commands.
